response.text:
## REFLECTION

The household r1c3 is in a critical energy deficit state within an islanded microgrid cluster where all peers are simultaneously rejecting outbound transfers due to their own reserve constraints. Current SoC of 14.67 kWh (37.6% of 39 kWh capacity) is declining under a 1.00 kW load, with an estimated autonomy of ~14.7 hours before depletion—well below safe operating margins. The memory log reveals a consistent pattern: r0c3, r1c2, and r2c3 have all rejected outbound requests citing their own minimum thresholds (55–70% SoC), while r0c3 has made repeated urgent requests that r1c3 cannot safely fulfill. One recent acceptance from r0c3 suggests some peers may be willing to receive if urgency is high, but the broader cluster dynamic indicates mutual scarcity and defensive posturing. With no trust circles established and no grid restoration signal, r1c3 must adopt strict self-preservation: minimize outbound sharing, aggressively solicit inbound energy from any willing donor, and prepare for potential load-shedding if SoC continues to decline. The islanded condition and peer rejections indicate this is a genuine microgrid deadlock requiring conservative resource hoarding.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.55
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.1
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded cluster in mutual energy deficit; r1c3 SoC 37.6% and declining. All peers rejecting outbound transfers. Prioritize inbound reception and self-preservation. Resume sharing only if SoC exceeds 60% or grid restoration confirmed. Prepare load-shedding contingency if SoC falls below 20%."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
